description: A complete networking course covering fundamentals, protocols, layers, and modern networking concepts for BCA students.
subject: Computer Networking

modules.title: Introduction to Computer Networks
topics: What is Computer Networking? Definition, purpose, and real-world examples (internet, banking, mobile networks)
Importance of networking in modern communication and data sharing
Basic components of a network: sender, receiver, transmission medium, protocol
Types of networks: LAN, MAN, WAN with real-life use cases
Star Topology: structure, working principle, advantages, disadvantages, real-world usage
Bus Topology: structure, working principle, advantages, disadvantages, limitations
Ring Topology: token passing mechanism, advantages, disadvantages, use cases
Mesh Topology: full vs partial mesh, reliability, cost analysis, applications
Hybrid Topology: combination of multiple topologies, practical implementation in organizations
Client-server architecture: concept, advantages, real-world examples (web server, database server)
Peer-to-peer architecture: concept, advantages, limitations, comparison with client-server
Switch: MAC address-based forwarding and collision reduction
Router: IP-based routing and network interconnection
Gateway: protocol conversion and communication between different networks
learningOutcomes: Differentiate types of networks and topologies

modules.title: OSI & TCP/IP Models
topics: Introduction to Layered Architecture: Why networking needs layers, complexity reduction, modular design benefits
Concept of Protocols, Services, and Interfaces: How layers communicate, service access points (SAP), and protocol rules
OSI Model Overview: History, purpose, and standardization by ISO
• Physical Layer – transmission of raw bits, cables, signals, hardware devices
• Data Link Layer – framing, MAC addressing, error detection, switches
• Network Layer – logical addressing (IP), routing, routers
• Transport Layer – end-to-end delivery, TCP vs UDP, reliability, flow control
• Presentation Layer – data translation, encryption, compression
• Application Layer – user-level services (HTTP, FTP, SMTP, DNS)
Encapsulation & Decapsulation Process: Step-by-step data flow across layers with headers and trailers
Data Units in Each Layer: Bits, Frames, Packets, Segments (important exam concept)
TCP/IP Model Overview: Origin, practical implementation, and Internet foundation
• Internet Layer – IP addressing and routing
• Application Layer – protocols and user services
Mapping OSI Layers to TCP/IP Model: Layer-wise correspondence and simplification
Key Differences Between OSI and TCP/IP Models: Structure, design philosophy, practical usage
Real-World Analogy: Postal system / courier service to explain layered communication
Common Protocols in Each Layer: HTTP, HTTPS, FTP, TCP, UDP, IP, ARP, ICMP
Devices Used in Different Layers: Hub, Switch, Router, Gateway
Advantages of Layered Models: Interoperability, scalability, troubleshooting ease
Limitations and Criticism of OSI Model: Theoretical vs practical gap
Real-Life Data Flow Example: How a web request travels from browser to server and back

modules.title: Test OSI Layer
learningOutcomes: Use pointers to work with memory addresses
Apply pointer arithmetic to traverse arrays
Understand the connection between arrays and pointers in C
modules.summary: Master the basics of pointers, the core feature that makes C powerful and close to hardware.
miniProjects: Practice: Pointers and Memory Addressing - Mini Project 1

modules.title: Transmission Media
topics: Introduction to Physical Layer: Role in OSI model and data transmission
Analog vs Digital Signals: Continuous vs discrete signals, waveform characteristics (amplitude, frequency, phase), advantages and limitations
Signal Impairments: Noise, attenuation, distortion, interference and their impact on data transmission
• Twisted Pair Cable: UTP vs STP, categories (Cat5, Cat6), uses in LAN
• Coaxial Cable: Structure, shielding, applications (cable TV, broadband)
• Optical Fiber: Core, cladding, types (single-mode, multi-mode), advantages (high speed, low loss)
• Microwave Transmission: Line-of-sight communication, terrestrial and satellite
• Satellite Communication: GEO, MEO, LEO satellites, working principle
• Infrared Communication: Short-range communication, remote controls
• LAN cabling in offices and schools

modules.title: Encoding, Multiplexing & Switching
topics: Encoding techniques: NRZ, Manchester, Differential Manchester
